zh.Wrawby, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾福拉比
zh.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches於1166年出生在英國林可爾史爾。他於1190年與Mrs. William de Arches在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾福拉比結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.Wrawby, Lincolnshire, England: 英国林可爾史爾福拉比
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches于1166年出生在英国林可爾史爾。他于1190年与Mrs. William de Arches在英国英格兰林可爾史爾福拉比结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Arches
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  Norman, English: locative name from any of various places in France called Arques (Eure, Pas-de-Calais, Seine-Maritime). The name was sometimes reduced to   Arch  .
2 English: variant of Arch with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Arch
meanings: English: topographic name for someone living by a bridge, from Middle English, Old French arche ‘arch’ (i.e. of a bridge).
English (of Norman origin): shortened form of Arches, a habitational name from any of various places in France called Arques (Eure, Pas-de-Calais, Seine-Maritime).
Germanized form of Slovenian Arh: from a shortened form of the personal name Areh, an old vernacular form of Henrik (see Henry 1).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches was born in 1166, in Lincolnshire, England. He married Mrs. William de Arches in 1190, in Wrawby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches est né en 1166, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Mrs. William de Arches en 1190, à Wrawby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches wurde 1166 in Lincolnshire, England geboren. Er hat 1190 in Wrawby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Mrs. William de Arches geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches è nato il nel 1166 nel Lincolnshire, England. Lui sposato Mrs. William de Arches nel 1190, nel Wrawby, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches(은)는 1166년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났다. 그는 1190년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 롤비에서 Mrs. William de Arches(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches nasceu em 1166, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Mrs. William de Arches em 1190, em Wrawby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches родился в 1166 г. в местности линколншир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. William de Arches в 1190 г. в местности равби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Sir William de Arches nació en 1166, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Mrs. William de Arches en 1190, en Wrawby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
